content:
A practical comparison based on workload, performance, and developer productivity — not hype.

5 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Google AI studio by AuthorEvery few months, a new benchmark makes the rounds.
Someone loads a few gigabytes of Parquet files, runs the same query through three different engines, and declares a winner.
A week later another benchmark reaches the opposite conclusion.
I’ve stopped paying much attention.
Benchmarks are useful when you’re evaluating algorithms. They’re much less useful when you’re deciding how an engineering team should process data for the next three years.
I’ve worked with Spark clusters that processed petabytes every week, DuckDB powering analytical tooling on developer laptops, and Polars becoming the backbone of Python data pipelines that had quietly outgrown pandas.
None of them replaced the others.
What changed over time wasn’t my opinion about which tool was fastest.
It was my understanding of the problems each one was actually trying to solve.
1. I Started by Comparing Performance
